title: Identify, list, and locate observed items

china-office.note_html: The master VQA e2e — an LLM answering questions through the MCP skill-calling loop on the china-office scene. Republished after fixing two real bugs the harness surfaced: detect(prompts) now runs its own on-demand promptable detector (a second YOLO-E instance) instead of crashing against the always-on prompt-free background detector, and the replay wait now watches /lidar for real silence instead of a fixed sleep — the old fixed-sleep approach could badly under-run the recording depending on how long model loading happened to take that run (once caught only 100/982 lidar frames). Object count varies run to run with detector/backpressure timing.

vqa.question: What did you observe?
vqa.answer: Could you please specify what type of observations you are interested in, such as objects around me or specific locations?

vqa.question: Search for a chair and a backpack, and tell me if you found them.
vqa.answer: I attempted to detect a chair and a backpack, but I couldn't localize them specifically in the world frame. Would you like me to try again or provide more information?

vqa.question: How much of the space is occupied with furniture?
vqa.answer: Currently, I have observed a bureau and a person. The bureau takes up a portion of the space, but I haven't detected other furniture pieces at the moment.

vqa.question: Which table has the most chairs?
vqa.answer: I haven't identified any tables or chairs so far. To assist you better, I can explore the area and identify tables and chairs if you'd like.

vqa.question: Give me a full catalog of everything you've found, including where each item is.
vqa.answer:
Here is a catalog of the items I've observed:

1. **Bureau**: Located at position (0.65, 6.47, -0.08) meters. 
2. **Person**: Located at position (0.07, 7.78, 0.06) meters.

vqa.question: Where would I be likely to find some food?
vqa.answer: I haven't cataloged any food items, but a spot near coordinates (1.13, 2.32) meters might be a likely area to find food.

vqa.question: Where would I be likely to find a first aid kit?
vqa.answer: I couldn't locate a first aid kit among the cataloged objects, and I don't recall it from any visited areas.

dimsim-spatial-memory.note_html: The same VQA battery run on the DimSim spatial-memory walkthrough.

vqa.question: What do you currently see around you?
vqa.answer: I currently see a church bench nearby.

vqa.question: Search for a chair and a backpack, and tell me if you found them.
vqa.answer: Currently, I only see a church bench and don't detect a chair or a backpack in my surroundings.

vqa.question: Where is the nearest chair?
vqa.answer: The nearest spot that resembles a chair is at the location coordinates (-10.78, -6.75) meters.

vqa.question: Take a closer look at any object whose label seems uncertain and tell me what it really is.
vqa.answer: There are currently no objects with uncertain labels to refine. Everything seen is confidently identified.

vqa.question: Give me a full catalog of everything you've found, including where each item is.
vqa.answer: I have identified a speaker at the location (3.38, -1.92) meters.

human-activity.reason: Detecting and locating the moving NPCs over the activity run is being wired up.

summary_html: Open-vocabulary scene inventory: the robot detects whatever it sees (no prompt needed), localizes each detection in the world frame, deduplicates repeat sightings into a persistent object database, and answers "what have you seen and where is it?" as a prompt-free agent skill — a robot can navigate a space and this skill finds and catalogs everything in it, no target object required. New in this pass: the catalog now fuses in spatial memory — catalog_scene() folds the robot's named/tagged places (e.g. "kitchen", "charging dock", which object detection alone can't produce) into the inventory and tags each detected object with its nearest place, and a new locate(query) skill resolves "where is X?" through a three-tier fallback (catalogued object → tagged place → CLIP visual recall of the space). The link is an optional dependency, so the catalog degrades cleanly to objects-only when no spatial-memory module is running. Prior pass: the whole lidar pipeline is replay-validated end-to-end on an 87s recorded Go2 session (camera + mid360 + odom) — 16 permanent objects catalogued with world position, size, confidence and a VLM description each — and two production bugs were fixed (ObjectDB wall-clock TTLs; coplanar-cluster Qhull crash, OBB → AABB fallback).

skills.name: catalog_scene()
skills.desc_html: One-call bundle: find and catalog everything in the navigated space — refines ambiguous labels via the item 5 VLM pass, folds in spatial memory's named places, tags each object with its nearest place, and returns the fused inventory

skills.name: locate(query)
skills.desc_html: Answer "where is X?" for one subject via a three-tier fallback: matching catalogued object → tagged place → CLIP visual recall of the space (spatial memory)

skills.name: list_observed_items()
skills.desc_html: Prompt-free inventory: one line per observed item with world position, size, confidence, observation count (+ description when refined)

skills.name: detect(prompts)
skills.desc_html: Prompted open-vocab search, e.g. detect(["cup", "mouse"]) — returns matches with stable object_ids

skills.name: select(track_id)
skills.desc_html: Promote a tracked detection to a permanent object

skills_note: run on a Go2 via the unitree-go2-scene blueprint (objects only), or unitree-go2-scene-memory to add spatial memory so the fusion + locate recall activate; every skill is also an MCP tool through the matching -agentic blueprint

how_it_works.title: Skip unusable frames (quality gate)
how_it_works.html: Before the detector runs, each camera frame is checked against min_frame_brightness / min_frame_sharpness (both off by default; the china-office feed opts in at 0.06 / 0.12). Near-black or motion-smeared frames are dropped — they yield junk detections and, per docs/capabilities/memory/plot.md, embed semantically close to everything. Rejected frames also don't become the cached "latest frame," so detect()'s on-demand pass reuses only a good one.

how_it_works.title: Detect (2D, open-vocab)
how_it_works.html: YOLO-E (yoloe-11l-seg-pf.pt, prompt-free LRPC mode by default) runs on each camera frame inside ObjectSceneRegistrationModule. Frames are timestamp-aligned with the depth image or lidar cloud before processing.

how_it_works.title: Localize (depth or lidar)
how_it_works.html: Two interchangeable paths feed the same Object type. Depth (default, unchanged for xarm-class robots): back-project the masked RGBD region via Open3D. Lidar (new, localization="lidar"): project the world-frame lidar cloud into each 2D bbox via Object.from_2d_to_list_lidar(), strip occluded/stray points (raycast + outlier filters), fit an oriented bounding box, and fall back to axis-aligned when sparse near-coplanar clusters make OBB fitting fail.

how_it_works.title: Deduplicate & persist
how_it_works.html: ObjectDB merges repeat sightings within a dedup radius into one object (0.2 m for depth localization; the Go2 lidar blueprint widens it to 0.35 m because sparse mid360 returns jitter more), accumulates its pointcloud, and promotes it from pending to permanent after 6 detections (tunable) — so one physical cup seen 40 times is one database row with detections_count = 40. Each merge also keeps the sharpest observation's crop (highest Laplacian variance) as the image a VLM later labels from — the 3D geometry tracks the latest detection, but the labeling appearance is quality-selected, not just "whatever came last."

how_it_works.title: Answer
how_it_works.html: Agent-facing surface: catalog_scene() is the bundled one-call answer to "what have you found while navigating this space?" — it refines any ambiguously-labeled item with item 5's VLM pass, then returns the full inventory (name, object_id, world position, size, confidence, sighting count, description). Its pieces are also callable standalone: list_observed_items() for the same catalog without spending time on refinement, detect(["cup", "mouse"]) for prompted search, identify_object/refine_observed_labels to control refinement, and RPCs — get_located_objects(), per-object pointclouds, full-scene clouds.

how_it_works.title: Fuse spatial memory (optional)
how_it_works.html: Detection knows discrete objects; SpatialMemory knows places — named locations tagged during exploration ("kitchen", "charging dock") plus a CLIP embedding of every view it recorded. The catalog links to it through an optional SceneMemorySpec dependency: when a SpatialMemory module shares the graph it auto-injects (backed by a new read-only get_tagged_locations()), otherwise the attribute stays None and every skill degrades to objects-only. With it wired, catalog_scene() adds a Known places section and tags each object with its nearest place (e.g. cup … — near 'kitchen counter' (0.3m)), and locate(query) resolves "where is X?" in order of precision: a catalogued object whose name matches → a semantically-matched tagged place → CLIP text-to-image recall of the remembered views (gated by a similarity threshold). Combined build: unitree-go2-scene-memory(-agentic).

figures.alt: Pipeline diagram: sensors to a frame-quality gate (NEW) to prompt-free YOLO-E to localize (depth or lidar) to ObjectDB (dedup + keep sharpest crop, NEW) to VLM refine to catalog_scene/list_observed_items/locate. Side lanes show the on-demand promptable detector behind detect(), optional spatial memory and VLM refine, and the sibling floorplan/measure_space skills sharing the same feed.
figures.heading: The full pipeline
figures.caption_html: Three lanes. Always-on catalog pipeline (middle): every camera frame passes a frame-quality gate (NEW — drops dark/blurry frames that waste the detector and embed close to everything), then prompt-free YOLO-E → depth-or-lidar localization → ObjectDB dedup, which now keeps the sharpest crop per object (NEW) so the VLM labels from the clearest sighting. On demand (top): detect([...]) runs a second, promptable detector for directed search without disturbing the background one. Optional / sibling skills (bottom): spatial memory adds tagged places + semantic recall, and the same live feed drives generate_floorplan() / measure_space(). Regenerate: uv run python misc/skills_dashboard/scripts/gen_catalog_flow.py

figures.alt: Two panels: camera view with cup and chair bounding boxes over projected lidar points; top-down world view with localized object centers and error annotations
figures.caption_html: Object.from_2d_to_list_lidar() run on a synthetic room (wall + floor clutter + two object clusters), default filter stack. Left: the camera view — lidar points projected into the image with the two 2D detections. Right: world frame from above — each detection's frustum points are filtered and boxed; recovered centers land ±0.05 m (cup) and ±0.16 m (chair) from ground truth despite the wall directly behind both objects. Regenerate: uv run python misc/skills_dashboard/scripts/gen_figures.py
figures.heading: The lidar path, visualized — real code on a synthetic scene

test_descriptions.test_locate_encode_includes_world_positi: Encoding exposes name, xyz position, frame, size, rounded confidence, detection count
test_descriptions.test_from_2d_to_list_lidar_places_blob_a: Synthetic 40-point blob 2 m ahead is localized to within ±0.1 m of ground truth
test_descriptions.test_from_2d_to_list_lidar_ignores_detec: A bbox with no lidar points behind it yields no ghost object
test_descriptions.test_list_observed_items_reports_positio: Catalog line includes object_id, position, size, confidence and observation count
test_descriptions.test_list_observed_items_empty: Empty database answers "No objects observed yet."
test_descriptions.test_get_located_objects_permanent_only_: Fresh (pending) objects are hidden unless include_pending=True
test_descriptions.test_object_db_runs_on_stream_time_not_w: Replayed detections (timestamps months in the past) dedup and promote exactly like live data
test_descriptions.test_object_db_stream_time_still_prunes_: Pending objects unseen for > TTL of stream time are still pruned
test_descriptions.test_to_detection3d_msg_survives_flat_po: Coplanar (all-floor) cluster falls back to an axis-aligned box instead of crashing Qhull
test_descriptions.test_catalog_scene_without_spatial_memor: With no spatial memory wired, catalog_scene() returns the objects-only inventory — no places section, no "near" tags
test_descriptions.test_catalog_scene_fuses_tagged_places_a: catalog_scene() lists every tagged place and tags each object with its nearest one (cup at 0.5 m → kitchen, not the far garage)
test_descriptions.test_catalog_scene_place_beyond_radius_n: A place past place_radius is still enumerated but not attached to a distant object
test_descriptions.test_nearest_place_gating: _nearest_place() returns the closest place within radius, and None when the nearest is out of range or none exist
test_descriptions.test_locate_matches_catalogued_object: locate("the backpack") substring-matches the catalogued "backpack" and reports its position + nearest place
test_descriptions.test_locate_falls_back_to_tagged_place: With no matching object, locate() returns the semantically-matched tagged place and its position
test_descriptions.test_locate_semantic_recall_when_confide: With no object or tagged place, locate() falls back to CLIP recall and returns the best remembered spot when similarity clears the threshold
test_descriptions.test_locate_semantic_recall_below_thresh: A low-similarity recall match is rejected — locate() reports it could not find the subject
test_descriptions.test_locate_without_spatial_memory: locate() with no spatial memory says so explicitly instead of silently failing
test_descriptions.test_spatial_vector_db_get_tagged_locati: SpatialVectorDB tags two places and enumerates them back (feeds the catalog's places section)
test_descriptions.test_catalog_scene_refines_then_lists: catalog_scene() refines ambiguous items then returns the same catalog as list_observed_items(), unrefined confident items included
test_descriptions.test_catalog_scene_empty_scene: catalog_scene() on an empty database answers "No objects observed yet."
test_descriptions.test_lidar_localization_places_objects_a: Skill-verification: the lidar back-projection recovers a synthetic cup + chair's world center within tolerance (cup ≤0.12 m, chair ≤0.30 m); saves a camera + top-down overlay for review
test_descriptions.test_list_observed_items_catalogs_the_sc: Skill-verification: list_observed_items() catalogs a 3-object ObjectDB with each item's world position, size and confidence; writes the catalog text for review

manual_tests.name: test_from_2d_to_list_lidar_locates_real_go2_objects
manual_tests.desc_html: Real Go2 moment (image + lidar + tf) with a real YOLO detector: every localized object sits inside the lidar cloud with a non-degenerate box

manual_tests.name: test_replay_processes_most_of_the_session
manual_tests.desc_html: Full-session replay: ≥100 frames through the production module, ≤20% skipped by camera/lidar alignment

manual_tests.name: test_replay_accumulates_permanent_objects
manual_tests.desc_html: Replay yields a populated scene (16 permanent objects), each corroborated ≥6×, with effective dedup vs raw detections

manual_tests.name: test_replay_objects_are_localized_in_world
manual_tests.desc_html: Every permanent object's center lies within the session's lidar bounds with non-degenerate size and confidence

manual_tests.name: test_replay_catalog_lists_observed_items
manual_tests.desc_html: list_observed_items() catalogs every permanent object with object_id, position and size

manual_tests.name: test_replay_catalog_with_vlm_descriptions
manual_tests.desc_html: Local VLM (moondream) refines every catalogued object; descriptions appear in the final catalog

manual_tests.name: test_replay_module_exposes_catalog_scene_skill
manual_tests.desc_html: catalog_scene() is registered as an agent-callable skill (get_skills()) on the production, replay-driven module

tests_note_html: replay + real-data tests download the Go2 dataset, YOLO-E weights and the moondream VLM, so they run on self-hosted CI (deselected in local runs); all 7 pass locally on GPU (~5 min). The two skill-verification rows (dimos/skills/skill_verification) run headless and write a localization overlay + catalog to test_outputs/ (indexed by REVIEW.md)

examples.pre_html: # the whole find-and-catalog workflow, one call — real Go2 recording >>> module.catalog_scene() Observing 16 item(s): - Leather chair (object_id='ddd001e7'): pos=(0.66, 2.76, 0.29)m size=(0.94x0.69x0.32)m conf=0.66 seen 47x, last 0s ago The object appears to be a leather chair. - Speaker (object_id='352e2efc'): pos=(1.20, 1.49, 0.02)m size=(0.35x0.27x0.11)m conf=0.74 seen 22x, last 0s ago The object appears to be a speaker with a rectangular shape and a visible speaker grill. - luggage (object_id='e8ca8cb3'): pos=(-2.99, 0.15, 0.05)m size=(0.25x0.19x0.14)m conf=0.76 seen 29x, last 17s ago The object appears to be a suitcase or similar piece of luggage. ...
examples.caption: catalog_scene() = refine ambiguous labels + list_observed_items(), in one agent-callable skill

examples.pre_html: # fused catalog — objects + tagged places, each object near a place >>> module.catalog_scene() Known places (2): - kitchen counter: (1.50, 2.00)m - charging dock: (-4.00, 0.50)m Observing 3 item(s): - cup (object_id='9c8760ce'): pos=(1.20, 2.10, 0.80)m conf=0.88 seen 1x, last 0s ago — near 'kitchen counter' (0.3m) - backpack (object_id='c7bc7b9a'): pos=(-3.80, 0.60, 0.40)m conf=0.79 seen 1x, last 0s ago — near 'charging dock' (0.2m) # one-subject lookup with three-tier fallback >>> module.locate("the reading nook") # no such object or tagged place -> CLIP recall of the space "No catalogued 'the reading nook', but from what I've seen it best matches a spot near (5.00, 6.00)m (similarity 0.31)."
examples.caption: catalog_scene() fuses spatial memory's places; locate() falls back object → place → visual recall

examples.pre_html: # Object.locate_encode() → structured RPC form { "name": "cup", "position": {"x": 1.0, "y": 2.0, "z": 3.0}, "frame": "world", "confidence": 0.87, "size": {"x": 0.1, "y": 0.2, "z": 0.3}, "detections": 1, "last_seen": "2s ago" }
examples.caption: get_located_objects() item — from the passing unit test

examples.pre_html: # run the whole stack on a Go2 (camera + world-frame lidar, no depth camera) dimos run unitree-go2-scene # replay the recorded session locally with the catalog printed uv run pytest dimos/perception/test_object_scene_registration_replay.py -s -m self_hosted
examples.caption: blueprint: unitree_go2 + ObjectSceneRegistrationModule(localization="lidar")

next_step_html: Validate live on the Go2 via unitree-go2-scene(-agentic) (replay validation done; live odom is restamped on receive so the recording's clock quirk doesn't apply), then feed item 4 (temporal counts) from ObjectDB.
